---
title: "storey_pi0"
generated: true
description: "Storey's estimate of the true-null proportion $ pi_0$."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.inference.multiple_comparisons`](../nitrix-stats-inference-multiple_comparisons.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:storey_pi0 -->
```python
storey_pi0(p_values: Float[Array, 'n'], *, lam: float = 0.5) -> Float[Array, '']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:storey_pi0 -->
Storey's estimate of the true-null proportion $\pi_0$.

Computes
$\hat{\pi}_0(\lambda) = \#\{p_i > \lambda\} / (m (1 - \lambda))$,
clipped to $[1/m, 1]$, where $m$ is the number of p-values. The
tuning parameter $\lambda \in (0, 1)$ (default `0.5`) trades bias
for variance. A value $\pi_0 = 1$ recovers Benjamini-Hochberg, and a
smaller $\pi_0$ (more alternatives present) yields a less conservative,
higher-power adaptive FDR. The $1/m$ floor guards against the degenerate
"no null" estimate.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:storey_pi0 -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `p_values` | `Float` | `n` | — | Flat vector of `n` per-element p-values. |
| `lam` | `float` | — | `0.5` | Tuning parameter $\lambda \in (0, 1)$ at which the null proportion is estimated. Must lie strictly within `(0, 1)`. Default `0.5`. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape —. The estimated true-null proportion $\pi_0$, a scalar clipped to $[1/m, 1]$.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:storey_pi0 -->
### References

Storey, J. D. (2002). A direct approach to false discovery rates. Journal of
the Royal Statistical Society: Series B (Statistical Methodology), 64(3),
479-498. https://doi.org/10.1111/1467-9868.00346
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:storey_pi0 -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
